⦗OSS CAD Suite⦘ (venv-church) sipantichijk@penguin:~/church-machine$ cd ~/church-machine/hardware/soc_combined

# ── D1. Check ALL FOUR symbol lanes' INIT_0 (not just symbol3) ──────────────
for sym in 0 1 2 3; do
  echo "=== symbol${sym} ==="
  LINENUM=$(grep -n "EFX_RAM10" outflow/church_soc_cm.map.v \
    | grep "ram_symbol${sym}__D\\\$g1" | head -1 | cut -d: -f1)
  if [ -z "$LINENUM" ]; then
    echo "  [no g1 instance found for symbol${sym}]"
  else
    sed -n "${LINENUM},$((LINENUM+8))p" outflow/church_soc_cm.map.v \
      | grep "INIT_[0123]"
  fi
done

# ── D2. Raw byte read from BOTH UARTs (not line-based) ──────────────────────
# Reset the board first, then immediately check both ports for raw bytes
echo "Reading raw bytes from ttyUSB2 (5 second window)..."
python3 -c "
import serial, time
s = serial.Serial('/dev/ttyUSB2', 115200, timeout=5)
t0 = time.time()
data = s.read(200)
s.close()
print(f'ttyUSB2 @ 115200: {len(data)} bytes raw: {data.hex()[:80]}')
"

echo "Reading raw bytes from ttyUSB3 (5 second window)..."
python3 -c "
import serial
s = serial.Serial('/dev/ttyUSB3', 115200, timeout=5)
data = s.read(200)
s.close()
print(f'ttyUSB3 @ 115200: {len(data)} bytes raw: {data.hex()[:80]}')
"

# ── D3. Try 57600 baud on ttyUSB2 (in case clock divider reset value is used) ──
echo "Reading raw bytes from ttyUSB2 @ 57600 baud..."
python3 -c "
"rint(f'ttyUSB2 @ 57600: {len(data)} bytes raw: {data.hex()[:80]}')
=== symbol0 ===
            .RDATA({\u_sapphire/u_EfxSapphireSoc/system_ramA_logic_io_bus_rsp_payload_fragment_data [7]})) /* verific EFX_ATTRIBUTE_CELL_NAME=EFX_RAM10, READ_WIDTH=1, WRITE_WIDTH=1, ttyUSB2 @ 115200: 0 bytes raw: 
Reading raw bytes from ttyUSB3 (5 second window)...
